groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Page summary, type, audience, primary keyword, facts, goal, language, brand, URL.

groups.title: Search Focus
groups.description: Intent, supporting keywords, geography, and wording restrictions.

groups.title: Technical Settings
groups.description: Title branding, slug handling, indexing, and canonical URL.

groups.title: Social Preview
groups.description: Optional Open Graph / X Card metadata and preview image.

groups.title: Output Options
groups.description: Number of variants and special requirements.

variables.label: Page topic or summary
variables.placeholder: Describe what the page contains, offers, or explains.
variables.hint: Summarize the real page content so the metadata accurately represents it.

help.why: The model must understand the actual page before it can write accurate metadata.
help.example: Simple online invoice generator for freelancers
help.avoid: Metadata that describes content not on the page.

variables.label: Page type
variables.placeholder: Select the closest page type.
variables.hint: Page type changes the title structure, description focus, slug, and social metadata.

help.why: Different page types require different metadata priorities and search-intent treatment.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Example: Small business owners looking for simple invoicing software
variables.hint: Describe the people most likely to search for and benefit from this page.

help.why: Audience context determines which benefits, terms, and intent signals should be emphasized.
help.example: Example: Small business owners looking for simple invoicing software
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Primary keyword or query
variables.placeholder: Example: online invoice generator
variables.hint: Enter the main phrase the page should naturally target.

help.why: The primary query anchors the title, description, and slug without requiring keyword stuffing.
help.avoid: Keyword stuffing or unrelated head terms.

variables.label: Essential page facts
variables.placeholder: List real features, benefits, locations, prices, dates, or other facts that may be mentioned.
variables.hint: Only provide facts that are accurate and publicly supported by the page.

help.why: It supplies the factual basis and prevents the model from inventing claims or page details.
help.example: Free plan, PDF export, no invented features
help.avoid: Unverified prices, rankings, or guarantees.

variables.label: Primary page goal
variables.placeholder: Select the page’s main goal.
variables.hint: The goal influences the description angle and the strength of any action phrase.

help.why: Pages targeting the same keyword may require different metadata depending on their conversion purpose.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
variables.hint: Metadata will be written in this language and writing system.

help.why: The output language cannot always be safely inferred from a short topic or keyword.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Brand or site name
variables.placeholder: Example: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Used only when brand inclusion improves recognition or is explicitly requested.

help.why: Brand names cannot be invented and may materially affect title length and structure.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Page URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/page
variables.hint: Used for canonical and social tags when it is the intended public URL.

help.why: A real URL enables implementation-ready canonical and social metadata without fabrication.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Search intent
variables.placeholder: Let the model infer intent or choose one.
variables.hint: Use Auto unless the page deliberately targets a particular stage of the search journey.

help.why: Explicit intent can prevent informational and commercial pages from receiving the wrong metadata angle.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Supporting keywords
variables.placeholder: Add closely related phrases, one per line or separated by commas.
variables.hint: Only include relevant phrases that may naturally support the primary topic.

help.why: Supporting phrases can improve topical coverage when used selectively.
help.example: Add closely related phrases, one per line or separated by commas.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Geographic target
variables.placeholder: Example: Toronto, Canada or nationwide United Kingdom
variables.hint: Complete this only when location materially affects the page or search intent.

help.why: Local relevance must come from the user rather than being inferred or invented.
help.example: Example: Toronto, Canada or nationwide United Kingdom
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Keyword and wording notes
variables.placeholder: List required terms, preferred wording, or phrases that must not appear.
variables.hint: Combine important inclusion and exclusion instructions in one place.

help.why: It captures material language restrictions without creating several overlapping fields.
help.example: List required terms, preferred wording, or phrases that must not appear.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Brand in SEO title
variables.placeholder: Choose how the brand should be handled.
variables.hint: Auto includes the brand only when useful and space allows.

help.why: Brand inclusion affects title clarity, distinctiveness, and available length.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Slug preference
variables.placeholder: Select how the URL path should be handled.
variables.hint: Choose whether to create, shorten, revise, or preserve the current slug.

help.why: Slug recommendations differ significantly between new and already published pages.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Current slug or path
variables.placeholder: Example: /services/online-invoice-generator
variables.hint: Enter the existing path so it can be reviewed or preserved.

help.why: The current path is necessary only when the user wants it reviewed or retained.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Indexing preference
variables.placeholder: Use Auto unless you know the page should be restricted.
variables.hint: Controls whether a robots meta recommendation is included.

help.why: Indexing directives can materially change page visibility and should not be guessed casually.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Canonical URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/preferred-page
variables.hint: Complete this when the preferred canonical differs from or must override the page URL.

help.why: Canonical URLs are technical factual values that must never be fabricated.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Include social metadata
variables.placeholder: Blank
variables.hint: Enable this to generate Open Graph or X Card preview metadata.

help.why: Social tags are valuable for some pages but unnecessary for users who only need search metadata.
help.avoid: Inventing social image URLs.

variables.label: Social metadata platforms
variables.placeholder:
Open Graph
X Card
variables.hint: Open Graph supports broad social sharing; X Card adds X-specific tags. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Open Graph; X Card.

help.why: The selected platforms determine which social tags should be generated.
help.avoid: Requesting formats you will not implement.

variables.label: Social preview image URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/images/page-preview.jpg
variables.hint: Use an absolute public image URL; the model will not invent one.

help.why: Social preview images require an actual hosted URL rather than a generated filename.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Social image description
variables.placeholder: Describe what is visibly shown in the social preview image.
variables.hint: Describe only real visible content and avoid keyword stuffing.

help.why: A supplied image description supports accurate and accessible social metadata.
help.example: Describe what is visibly shown in the social preview image.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.placeholder: Choose how many title and description pairs to generate.
variables.hint: More variants provide alternative angles without changing the factual basis.

help.why: Users may need either one final answer or several options for review.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any final content, formatting, CMS, or implementation requirements.
variables.hint: Use this only for important instructions not already covered above.

help.why: It accommodates legitimate edge cases without expanding the permanent form.
help.example: Add any final content, formatting, CMS, or implementation requirements.
help.avoid: Invented page facts, fake rankings, keyword stuffing, or fabricated URLs.
